evals.prompt: I want to review a work item but I'm not sure which one to pick. How do I use the review-work item command?
evals.expected_output: Responds with help text explaining how to invoke the skill. Should mention providing a work item path or work item number shorthand, give an example invocation, and reference /list-work-items as a way to discover work items. Should NOT attempt to run a review.

assertions.text: shows an example invocation with a work item path or number
does not attempt to run a review or read any work item file
response is a help prompt rather than a question asking for more information

evals.prompt: Please review the work item at meta/work/9999-nonexistent-work item.md
evals.expected_output: Reports that the work item does not exist at the given path. Should use phrasing like 'No work item at meta/work/9999-nonexistent-work item.md' and offer to run /list-work-items to find a valid work item path. Should NOT attempt to run a review against a nonexistent file.

assertions.text: response contains 'No work item at' followed by the path
does not attempt to review or open the nonexistent file

evals.prompt:
Please review the work item at /Users/tobyclemson/Code/organisations/atomic/company/accelerator/workspaces/ticket-management/skills/work/review-work-item/evals/files/full-review-flow/work-item.md

Proceed with all three lenses without waiting for confirmation. After the review is complete and the review file is written, also copy the review file to the outputs directory at the path you were given for outputs.
evals.expected_output: Runs the full review flow: reads the work item, spawns three reviewer agents in parallel (completeness, testability, clarity), aggregates findings, determines a verdict, presents the review summary, and writes a review artifact. The review file should have correct frontmatter (type: work-item-review, skill: review-work-item, lenses listing all three). The work item is a well-structured story so verdict should be APPROVE or COMMENT. Per-Lens Results section should be present.

assertions.text: spawns three reviewer agents (completeness, testability, clarity)
review file written with correct frontmatter (type: work-item-review, skill: review-work-item)
all three lenses listed in the lenses frontmatter field
verdict is one of APPROVE, REVISE, or COMMENT
review contains a Per-Lens Results section
no critical findings for this well-structured work item
